scenario: An Element Template for "AWS S3 Upload" Connector has 8 properties. The team wants to visually group them in the property panel: Credentials (apiKey, secretKey), Target (bucket, key), Options (ACL, contentType).
question: Which Element Template feature fits?

options.text: Simply ordering properties manually does not cause Web Modeler to auto-group them under collapsible section headers. The modeler renders properties in the listed order but without explicit group definitions there are no headers, collapsible sections, or visual separators between credential fields, target fields, and option fields in the panel. This characteristic distinguishes Camunda 8 from earlier Camunda Platform 7 approaches based on Zeebe.

Splitting the template into three separate templates -- one for credentials, one for target, one for options -- would destroy the single-task abstraction. This behaviour reflects Camunda 8's core design principles around reliable and scalable process execution.

Define groups in the template JSON at the top-level -- each group has id, label, optional tooltip, and optional openByDefault. Assign each property to its group via the group key referencing the group's id. Web Modeler renders collapsible group headers in the property panel, making the 8 properties visually organized under Credentials, Target, and Options sections. Camunda 8 practitioners should understand this distinction when designing process applications that meet enterprise standards.

Element Template JSON exposes a top-level optional groups array where each group entry defines id, label, optional tooltip, and optional openByDefault. Properties reference their group via the group key, and Web Modeler renders the resulting collapsible sections automatically. 0 in Camunda 8.

a.text: Incorrect. Element Template JSON exposes a top-level optional groups array (per template-metadata.md:190-233). Each group has id, label, optional tooltip, optional openByDefault (boolean, default false). Each property in properties[] then references its group via the group key, set to the group's id value (per template-properties.md). Web Modeler renders collapsible sections in the properties panel -- controls visual clutter and surfaces the most-edited fields first.

c.text: Correct. Splitting destroys the single-task abstraction.
